title: libpod/container: Replace containerState* with containerPlatformState
body:
This way we don't need to stub in structures for other OSes (e.g. the Darwin stub in a Linux-only file).

@mheon was [concerned about errors unmarshalling, say, a Linux state object on a Windows box][1], but we [can address that in checks when loading the database][2].  I'm not familiar enough with Bolt to add that check easily, but I've added a FIXME where I think it would go.

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1015#discussion_r198649043
[2]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1015#discussion_r198802956

comments.body: @wking For the OS check - I would implement it very similar to https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/master/libpod/boltdb_state_internal.go#L66-L69 using `validateDBAgainstConfig()` but checking `runtime.GOOS`

> For the OS check...

Ok, I've taken a stab at that with 6af1af6a -> 7245d52d.  Let me know if you want any adjustments to what I did, I don't have strong opinions about where it should live in the Bolt database or when it should be written or checked.
@wking Can you run gofmt so the tests will pass?

> Can you run gofmt so the tests will pass?

My local gofmt says everything is fine ;).  I've filed #1038 porting a commit from CRI-O so that the Travis logs will include gofmt's suggested changes.
Rebased onto master with 912a5e83 -> 9958056e, so I'll be able to see the `gofmt` suggestions in the Travis logs.
LGTM but I think this will have a merge dance with #1047

That one needs some work prior to merge, so I'm going to get this one in now.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
